2606.25960.title: Agentic System as Compressor: Quantifying System Intelligence in Bits
2606.25960.paper_summary: Large language models are turning from isolated predictors into agentic systems: they call tools, retrieve evidence, obey environment constraints, use verifiers, and complete tasks through search and multi-turn interaction. We adopts an analytical viewpoint based on "compression is intelligence": under a fixed task distribution, interface, and compute budget, a stronger agentic system lets a target object be reconstructed with fewer bits. We operationalize the measure with arithmetic coding, seed coding, and a fallback, and evaluate it in five settings: reversed text, chess moves, protein sequences, retrieval-augmented question answering, and semantic story compression; in all of them agentic components reduce codelength. These small, controlled experiments cover component types typical of real agentic systems, show that codelength can analyze how components, observers, and budgets change residual uncertainty, and offer guidance for evaluating real agent systems.
2606.25960.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型正從孤立的預測者轉變為具有代理性的系統：它們呼叫工具、檢索證據、遵循環境約束、使用驗證器，並通過搜索和多輪互動來完成任務。我們採用基於「壓縮即智慧」的分析觀點：在固定的任務分佈、介面和計算預算下，更強的代理系統可以用更少的位元重建目標物體。我們通過算術編碼、種子編碼和後備方案來操作這一衡量標準，並在五個設定中進行評估：反向文本、棋步、蛋白質序列、檢索增強的問題回答和語義故事壓縮；在所有這些情況下，代理組件都減少了代碼長度。這些小型、受控的實驗涵蓋了典型的真實代理系統的組件類型，顯示代碼長度可以分析組件、觀察者和預算如何改變殘餘不確定性，並為評估真實代理系統提供指導。
2606.25960.author: Zihan Qin et.al.
